merger_name: Gunvor Group – ZEN Energy Retail Holdings

targets.name: ZEN ENERGY RETAIL HOLDINGS PTY LTD

anzsic_codes.name: On Selling Electricity and Electricity Market Operation

merger_description:
Gunvor Group Ltd, or a wholly-owned Australian subsidiary nominated by Gunvor Group Ltd, proposes to acquire by way of a share purchase agreement 100% of the issued share capital of ZEN Energy Retail Holdings Pty Ltd (the **Target**) from ZEN Energy Retail Pty Ltd (**ZER**) (the **Acquisition**).

The Target, which has been newly incorporated for the purpose of the Acquisition, will hold the assets relating to the "ZEN Energy" retail business, including all of ZER's retail electricity supply customer contracts and a hedging portfolio consisting of three power purchase agreements (**PPAs**) (relating to the Tailem Bend 2 Hybrid Project, New England Solar Farm and Moree Solar Farm) and a virtual toll, energy storage services agreement and other synthetic battery offtake agreement in respect of the Templers battery energy storage systems (**BESS**) facility, together with employees, intellectual property, licences, and IT systems. Prior to completion, ZER will transfer the assets within the sale perimeter to the Target.

Gunvor Group Ltd is the main holding company of the Gunvor Group (**Gunvor**), a global commodity trading house that is registered in Cyprus and has an energy trading business. While Gunvor does not currently operate a physical business in Australia, it is a supplier of fuel to Australian fuel importers and a participant in the wholesale electricity derivative contracts market within the National Electricity Market (**NEM**). Gunvor is not involved in the wholesale generation, or the retail supply, of electricity in the NEM or elsewhere in Australia, and it does not own or control any operating energy generation assets or infrastructure.

ZER (and its affiliates, together the **ZEN Energy Group**) is an Australian renewable energy company which supplies electricity to large use customers across the NEM. The ZEN Energy Group operates two business units: a retail customer business with an associated hedging portfolio, and an asset development program. Its hedging portfolio primarily comprises long-term wholesale derivative contracts, including PPAs with renewable generators, and tolling leases over operating BESS.

The Acquisition is limited to the ZEN Energy retail business and three PPAs and the tolling agreement within its hedging portfolio, and does not include the asset development business unit owned by Zen Energy Pty Ltd or the remainder of the hedging portfolio.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Gunvor Group Ltd applied for a notification waiver in respect of its
(or a wholly-owned Australian subsidiary nominated by Gunvor
Group Ltd) proposed acquisition of 100% of the issued share capital
of ZEN Energy Retail Holdings Pty Ltd (the Target) from ZEN Energy
Retail Pty Ltd (ZER), as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Gunvor Group Ltd, is the main holding company of the
Gunvor Group (Gunvor), a global commodity trading house that is
registered in Cyprus and has an energy trading business. While
Gunvor does not currently operate a physical business in Australia, it
is a supplier of fuel to Australian fuel importers and a participant in
the wholesale electricity derivative contracts market within the
National Electricity Market (NEM). Gunvor is not involved in the
wholesale generation, or the retail supply, of electricity in the NEM or
elsewhere in Australia, and it does not own or control any operating
energy generation assets or infrastructure.
ZER (and its affiliates, together the ZEN Energy Group) is an
Australian renewable energy company which supplies electricity to
large use customers across the NEM. The ZEN Energy Group
operates two business units: a retail customer business with an
associated hedging portfolio, and an asset development program. Its
hedging portfolio primarily comprises long-term wholesale derivative
contracts, including power purchase agreements (PPAs) with
renewable generators, and tolling leases over operating battery
energy storage systems (BESS).
The Target, which has been newly incorporated for the purpose of
the Acquisition, will hold the assets relating to the ZEN Energy
Group’s retail business, including all of ZER's retail electricity supply
customer contracts and a hedging portfolio consisting of three PPAs
(relating to the Tailem Bend 2 Hybrid Project, New England Solar
Farm and Moree Solar Farm) and a virtual toll, energy storage
services agreement and other synthetic battery offtake agreement in
respect of the Templers BESS facility, together with employees,
intellectual property, licences, and IT systems. Prior to completion,
ZER will transfer the assets within the sale perimeter to the Target.
The Acquisition is limited to the ZEN Energy retail business and three
PPAs and the tolling agreement within its hedging portfolio, and
does not include the asset development business unit owned by Zen
Energy Pty Ltd or the remainder of the hedging portfolio.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers
that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening
of competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap between Gunvor and the
Target in the retail supply of electricity to large use
customers in the NEM
b. post-Acquisition Gunvor’s share in the acquisition of
wholesale derivative contracts for renewable energy and
battery storage assets in the NEM and in Queensland, NSW,
Victoria, South Australia and Tasmania is estimated to be
low
c. there are numerous alternative retail suppliers of electricity
to large use customers, and alternative acquirers of
wholesale derivative contracts for renewable energy and
battery storage assets in the NEM and across Australia
d. there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other
concerning vertical effects resulting from exclusionary
conduct post-acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
